_comment: One file per procedure_code. Distilled from the matching pair in PM JAY/Standard Treatment Guideline/master/<condition>/ : _Medco.pdf (clinical + mandatory docs) + _PPD.CPD.pdf (PPD/CPD processing checklists). Filename = procedure_code. This is what the Documentation Checker, Query Predictor, and Medical Justification agents read AFTER the picker has chosen the code.
min_doctor_qualification: DNB or Equivalent (in Urology, Pediatric Surgery)
clinical_indications: Considered in patients who have renal anomalies.
Considered in patients who are poorly compliant.
Considered in patients who have a large single renal-pelvic calculus.

preauth.label: Clinical notes including evaluation findings, indication for procedure, and planned line of management
Noncontrast head CT (NCCT) + CT Intravenous Pyelogram (CT IVP) confirming the indication

claim.label: Post procedure X Ray / USG KUB showing the stone removed

ppd_preauth.q: Was Clinical notes including evaluation findings, indication for procedure, and planned line of management submitted?
Was the Noncontrast head CT (NCCT) + CT Intravenous Pyelogram (CT IVP) report submitted?
Was the Clinical notes and Noncontrast head CT (NCCT) + CT Intravenous Pyelogram (CT IVP) report indicative of procedure?

cpd_claim.q: Are the detailed Indoor case papers submitted?
Was the Detailed Procedure/Operative notes submitted?
Post procedure X Ray / USG KUB showing the stone removed along with Patient ID and Date submitted?
Was the Detailed discharge summary submitted?

common_queries: Clinical notes and/or the Noncontrast head CT (NCCT) / CT Intravenous Pyelogram (CT IVP) report are not indicative of Pyelolithotomy procedure.
Mandatory clinical notes including evaluation findings, indication for procedure, and planned line of management are missing or incomplete.
The Noncontrast head CT (NCCT) + CT Intravenous Pyelogram (CT IVP) report is missing or does not confirm the indication for Pyelolithotomy.
Post-procedure X-Ray or USG KUB not showing the stone removed, or missing Patient ID and Date.

clinical_key_pointers: Pyelolithotomy: is procedure of choice for stones within the renal pelvis, including stones that demonstrated minimal invasion into calyces and infundibulum.
After introducing extracorporeal shockwave lithotripsy (ESWL) and percutaneous nephropyelolithotomy (PCN), pyelolithotomy has become an uncommon surgery in most countries
additional_information.post_op_care: An imaging study to confirm the removal of all stone particle, prescribing drugs and Patient education.
